▼ Architecture & Changes

Architecture

▼
Click a zone to filter findings • Click background to reset
3
Blue circle = files changed in zone
Factory infrastructure
Product code
Infrastructure & docs
Click zone to filter • click background to reset

What Changed

▼

Generated from git diff by delegated diff-reading agent. Code diffs are ground truth.

Infrastructure: Major restructuring of the PR review pack from a 3-pass monolithic skill into a 4-phase pipeline (Setup, Review, Assemble, Deliver) with structured JSONL contracts between agents and a Pydantic validation layer. All 5 reviewer paradigm prompts rewritten for JSONL output. New synthesis reviewer added as 6th cross-cutting agent. Extensive design proposals, research documentation, and convergence plans added to docs/.
Product: No product code changes in this PR. All 59 changed files are in infrastructure and factory zones (review-pack, review-prompts, repo-infra, docs). The MiniPong RL system (environment, agent, training, dashboard) is untouched.

Review Pack

Core pipeline rewrite: new models.py (605 lines) defines Pydantic schemas for all agent outputs. New assemble_review_pack.py (1006 lines) validates and transforms JSONL into ReviewPackData. New review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) orchestrates Phase 1 scaffolding. New inspect_session.py (793 lines) provides 12-check session validation. render_review_pack.py expanded with Key Findings, corroboration detection, and File Coverage table rendering. 6 agent definition files added. 5 JSON schemas added (duplicated across two directories). E2E tests consolidated and expanded (+371 lines). New unit tests: test_assembler.py (744 lines), test_models.py (489 lines). Deleted obsolete e2e template and PR-specific spec.

Review Prompts

All 5 existing paradigm prompts rewritten to produce hybrid JSONL output (FileReviewOutcome per file + ReviewConcept findings). New synthesis_review.md prompt added for the cross-cutting 6th reviewer role.

Repo Infrastructure

8 design proposals and work plans added to docs/proposals/ documenting the v3 architecture, streamlining rationale, skill introspection plan, and convergence approach.

Documentation

New docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md research document. docs/CONVENTIONS.md updated with progress log convention.

Key Decisions

▼
1
Restructure review pack from 3-pass to 4-phase pipeline
The monolithic 3-pass approach coupled agent execution with rendering, making iteration and validation difficult. A 4-phase pipeline (Setup, Review, Assemble, Deliver) with explicit JSONL contracts between phases enables independent testing, parallel agent execution, and deterministic assembly.

The PR replaces the previous approach where the orchestrator performed setup, review, and rendering in interleaved passes with a strict phase separation. Each phase has a clear contract: Phase 1 (Setup) produces scaffolded directories and pre-created JSONL files; Phase 2 (Review) fills those JSONL files via 6 independent agents; Phase 3 (Assemble) validates and transforms JSONL into a unified data structure; Phase 4 (Deliver) renders HTML from that structure. This is documented in packages/pr-review-pack/docs/skill-flow.md and the rewritten SKILL.md.

review-pack review-prompts
FileChange
packages/pr-review-pack/SKILL.mdComplete rewrite defining 4-phase pipeline with agent team support
packages/pr-review-pack/docs/skill-flow.mdNew 339-line flow diagram documenting the pipeline architecture
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/review_pack_setup.pyNew Phase 1 orchestrator for checkout, diff, scaffolding
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/assemble_review_pack.pyNew Phase 3 assembler with validation and transforms
2
Pydantic models as single source of truth for agent output validation
JSON schemas are useful for agent prompting but cannot enforce cross-field invariants or complex validation rules. Pydantic models provide runtime enforcement with proper error messages, while JSON schemas serve as the agent-facing contract.

The PR introduces models.py (605 lines) with Pydantic models for all structured output types: ReviewConcept, FileReviewOutcome, ConceptUpdate, SemanticOutput, and ArchitectureAssessmentOutput. These models enforce kebab-case zone IDs, grade enums (A/B+/B/C/F, explicitly excluding N/A and D), required field constraints, and cross-field validation (e.g., output_type must match the populated field in SemanticOutput). The JSON schema files exist as reference for agents but Pydantic is the enforcement layer. Corroborated by adversarial reviewer confirming validators are real, not stubs.

review-pack
FileChange
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/models.pyNew Pydantic model layer with strict validation
packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_models.py489-line test suite covering all validation rules
packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/SemanticOutput.schema.jsonJSON schema reference for agent prompts
3
Decouple agent identity from paradigm prompts
Previous approach embedded agent configuration in paradigm prompts, making it impossible to change tooling permissions or team configuration without editing review methodology.

The PR introduces 6 agent definition files in packages/pr-review-pack/agents/ that define each reviewer's identity, tool permissions, and schema references. These reference paradigm prompts in the separate packages/review-prompts/ zone via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} path variables. This separation means review methodology (what to look for, how to grade) is independent of agent harness configuration (which tools to use, which schemas to validate against). The architecture reviewer confirmed this cross-zone coupling is intentional and well-bounded.

review-pack review-prompts
FileChange
packages/pr-review-pack/agents/architecture-reviewer.mdNew agent definition referencing paradigm prompt via path variable
packages/pr-review-pack/agents/synthesis-reviewer.mdNew agent definition for the 6th cross-cutting reviewer
packages/review-prompts/architecture_review.mdParadigm prompt rewritten for JSONL output format
4
Add synthesis reviewer as 6th cross-cutting agent
Individual reviewers produce per-file grades and domain-specific findings, but no single reviewer can identify cross-cutting patterns, corroboration between agents, or produce coherent high-level summaries.

The PR adds a synthesis reviewer that runs after the 5 specialist reviewers complete. It reads all reviewer JSONL outputs and the diff data to produce: (1) what-changed summaries split by infrastructure/product layer with per-zone breakdowns, (2) key decisions evident in the PR, (3) post-merge items with failure/success scenarios. The synthesis reviewer's output uses a distinct schema (SemanticOutput) with a discriminated union pattern. A new paradigm prompt at packages/review-prompts/synthesis_review.md defines the methodology.

review-pack review-prompts
FileChange
packages/review-prompts/synthesis_review.mdNew 105-line paradigm prompt for synthesis review methodology
packages/pr-review-pack/agents/synthesis-reviewer.mdAgent definition for synthesis reviewer
packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/SemanticOutput.schema.jsonSchema defining what_changed, decision, post_merge_item, factory_event output types

Architecture Assessment

▼
Healthy

This PR is a major but architecturally clean evolution of the review-pack system from a 3-pass to a 4-phase pipeline. The new architecture introduces proper separation of concerns: Pydantic models for validation, an assembler for transformation, independent agent definitions, and a structured JSONL contract between review agents and the assembler. Cross-zone coupling between review-pack and review-prompts is intentional and narrow. The only structural concern is duplicated schema files across two directories (references/schemas/ and schemas/) which creates a future maintenance risk. All files are zoned, though docs/research/ coverage depends on fnmatch glob behavior.

Architect’s Recommendations

▼
Cross-Zone Coupling
review-pack → review-prompts: Agent definitions reference paradigm prompts via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/review-prompts/ path. Coupling is intentional and well-bounded through JSONL schema contract.
Zone Registry
NIT docs: docs/research/** not explicitly covered — relies on fnmatch * matching / which is non-standard glob behavior

Key Findings Agent Teams

▼
F C B B+ A
TI Test IntegrityAD AdversarialAR ArchitectureCH Code HealthSE Security
No findings match the current filter.
GradeFindingAgentZoneCorr.
Cinspect_session.py (793 lines) has zero test coverageTIreview-pack1x

inspect_session.py is 793 lines of validation logic that serves as a quality gate for review sessions. It contains:

  • JSONL file discovery and parsing
  • Schema validation against Pydantic models
  • File coverage completeness checks
  • Concept backing verification (non-A grades need backing concepts)
  • Zone registry cross-referencing
  • Synthesis content validation

While some of this logic overlaps with assemble_review_pack.py (which is tested), the inspector has its own implementation with distinct error reporting. Without tests, regressions in the inspector could silently pass broken review sessions through the factory.

The assembler tests cover similar concepts but through different code paths — this is not a substitute for testing the inspector directly.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/inspect_session.py
Creview_pack_setup.py and check_prerequisites.py have no testsTIreview-pack1x

review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) handles:

  • Review directory creation and JSONL file scaffolding
  • Meta header generation for agent output files
  • Diff data generation orchestration

check_prerequisites.py (114 lines) handles:

  • Python version detection
  • Pydantic availability checking
  • Playwright installation verification

Both scripts perform I/O and environment detection that could be tested with tmp_path fixtures and mock environments. Without tests, silent failures in setup could produce empty or malformed JSONL files that downstream tools fail to process.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/review_pack_setup.py
BDuplicate schema files create silent drift riskADCHreview-pack2x

The PR adds identical schema files to two locations:

  • packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files)
  • packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files)

The SKILL.md and reviewer prompts reference references/schemas/, but the schemas/ directory also exists. If a future change updates one copy but not the other, the pydantic models (which are the true enforcement layer) may diverge from the JSON schemas that agents read. This is not gaming, but it creates a subtle integrity gap: an agent could pass JSON schema validation against a stale copy while the assembler rejects it via pydantic.

Recommendation: use symlinks or a single canonical location.

packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/ReviewConcept.schema.json
Corroborated by: ADCH
BLEGACY_GRADE_SORT_ORDER collapses F and C to same valueADreview-pack1x

In models.py, the legacy grade sort order is:

LEGACY_GRADE_SORT_ORDER = {Grade.F: 0, Grade.C: 0, Grade.B: 1, Grade.B_PLUS: 2, Grade.A: 3}

The assembler's transform_concepts_to_review() correctly handles this by using a secondary _GRADE_SEVERITY dict to break ties. The test test_overall_grade_f_not_suppressed_by_c explicitly validates this edge case. So the implementation is correct — but the dual-sort-order design is fragile.

If anyone uses LEGACY_GRADE_SORT_ORDER directly for sorting (without the secondary tiebreaker), F-grade findings could sort after C-grade findings or vice versa depending on insertion order. The fix is documented in test, which is good. Not gaming, but a latent integrity risk.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/models.py
Binstall.sh performs rm -rf on target directory without safety checkADreview-pack1x

The install script has:

elif [ -d "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    echo "  Removing old installation..."
    rm -rf "$INSTALL_DIR"
fi

INSTALL_DIR is hardcoded to $HOME/.claude/skills/pr-review-pack, so the blast radius is limited. However, the script doesn't check that the directory actually contains a SKILL.md or other marker before deleting. If someone has a symlink at that path pointing elsewhere, the -L check catches symlinks but rm -rf on a real directory is unconditional.

This is not gaming — it's a minor safety gap in a developer-only tool. The verification step after installation is a good compensating control.

packages/pr-review-pack/install.sh
BSchema files duplicated across references/schemas/ and schemas/ directoriesARreview-pack1x

Five schema files (ArchitectureAssessmentOutput, ConceptUpdate, FileReviewOutcome, ReviewConcept, SemanticOutput) are byte-identical copies in two directories. The references/schemas/ copy is referenced by agent paradigm prompts; the schemas/ copy appears to serve models.py and the assembler. If only one copy is updated in a future PR, schema divergence will cause silent validation mismatches.

Recommendation: Pick one canonical location and symlink or remove the other. Alternatively, have the Pydantic models in models.py export the schemas programmatically.

packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/ArchitectureAssessmentOutput.schema.json
Binspect_session.py check_filesystem_artifacts references html_files in scope guard incorrectlyCHreview-pack1x

In check_filesystem_artifacts at line 605:

"html_found": len(html_files) > 0 if 'html_files' in dir() else False,

This uses 'html_files' in dir() as a scope guard, which is a code smell. The variable html_files is only assigned inside the first if html_files: block (line 551), but it is actually always assigned at line 549 via list((repo_dir / "docs").glob(html_pattern)), so the guard is vestigial. However, using dir() for variable existence checks is fragile and non-Pythonic. A simple initialization like html_found = False at the top of the function would be clearer.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/inspect_session.py
Brender_review_pack.py HTML string construction at scale creates maintenance burdenCHreview-pack1x

The renderer builds complex HTML structures via Python f-string concatenation across multiple functions. Two functions stand out:

  • render_architecture_svg (lines 157-266, ~110 lines): Constructs SVG elements with inline style attributes, onclick handlers, and nested text/tspan elements.
  • render_architecture_assessment (lines 269-447, ~180 lines): Builds nested collapsible sections with tables, badges, and subsections via string list concatenation.

This pattern is functionally correct but creates a maintenance burden: HTML structure is interleaved with Python logic, making it difficult to verify tag balance, attribute quoting, or CSS class correctness. The template_v2.html file uses injection markers which partially addresses this, but the section renderers themselves are still pure string construction.

This is not a bug but a structural complexity that increases the risk of regressions in future changes to the HTML output.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/render_review_pack.py
BGraphQL query string interpolation without parameterizationSEreview-pack1x

In scaffold_review_pack_data.py (lines 691-699) and review_pack_setup.py (lines 85-93), the GitHub owner and repo name are extracted from git remote get-url origin and interpolated directly into GraphQL query strings:

query=f'{{ repository(owner: "{owner}", name: "{name}") {{ ... }} }}'

Risk assessment: Low severity in this context. The values come from the local git remote URL, which is controlled by the repository configuration. GitHub's repository naming rules ([a-zA-Z0-9._-]) constrain the character set, making injection impractical. The code in scaffold_review_pack_data.py (line 688) documents this trust assumption explicitly.

Nevertheless, the idiomatic approach is to use GraphQL variables ($owner: String!) passed via the -F flag to gh api graphql, which would eliminate the interpolation entirely and be more robust against edge cases (e.g., repo names containing quotes, which GitHub currently disallows but the code doesn't validate).

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/scaffold_review_pack_data.py
BShell scripts use destructive operations and CLI-arg-derived pathsSEreview-pack1x

install.sh (lines 16-22): Uses rm -rf "$INSTALL_DIR" where INSTALL_DIR is hardcoded to $HOME/.claude/skills/pr-review-pack. This is safe — the path is a constant, not derived from user input. The script also checks whether the target is a symlink vs directory before choosing the removal method.

validate_skill.sh (lines 79-88): Uses rm -rf docs/reviews/ docs/*review_pack*.html zone-registry.yaml and git checkout -- . && git clean -fd inside cloned repo directories. The target directories are constructed from CLI-provided owner/repo:PR specs via dir_name=$(echo "$repo" | tr '/' '-')-${pr}. The IFS=: read -r repo pr pattern correctly splits the spec.

Additionally, validate_skill.sh Step 4 (lines 155-173) embeds $result_file and $dir_name into an inline Python script via shell interpolation. These variables are derived from the CLI spec and results directory, not from external input, so injection risk is negligible.

Risk assessment: Low. All destructive operations target paths that are either hardcoded constants or constructed from developer-provided CLI arguments in a local development context. The scripts use set -euo pipefail for fail-fast behavior. No untrusted input reaches these operations.

packages/pr-review-pack/install.sh
BNew renderer functions lack direct unit testsTIreview-pack1x

The renderer expansion adds three significant new functions:

  • render_key_findings() — groups findings by severity, renders corroboration badges, collapses A-grade findings
  • _detect_corroboration() — detects cross-agent corroboration via file overlap and title similarity
  • render_file_coverage_table() — renders per-agent grade columns for each file

The e2e tests in review-pack-v2.spec.ts verify these render correctly in the browser, which is valuable. However, unit tests would catch edge cases: empty findings list, single agent with no corroboration possible, title similarity false positives, and grade sorting with mixed severity.

The existing test_renderer_sidebar.py and test_renderer_integration.py updates only cover the nav rename from 'Code Review' to 'Key Findings'/'File Coverage' — they do not test the new rendering logic.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/render_review_pack.py
BUntested orchestration and validation scripts (validate_skill.sh, install.sh, run_deterministic_review.py, scaffold_review_pack_data.py)TIreview-pack1x

Four scripts in this PR have B-grade test coverage gaps:

  • validate_skill.sh (290 lines) — shell script running validation checks across the review pack pipeline. Contains conditional logic for check sequencing and error aggregation with no test harness.
  • install.sh (74 lines) — environment detection and package installation. Silent failures here could leave the skill non-functional.
  • run_deterministic_review.py (272 lines) — orchestrates deterministic review steps by calling other scripts. The called scripts are individually tested but the orchestration error paths are not.
  • scaffold_review_pack_data.py (refactored, +116/-77) — significant logic changes to scaffolding with no test updates. Existing integration tests may catch gross failures but refactored branching logic is unvalidated.

These are graded B rather than C because they are primarily orchestration scripts that delegate to tested components. The untested surface is control flow and error handling, not core business logic.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/validate_skill.sh
B+Duplicate schema directories: schemas/ and references/schemas/ are byte-identicalCHADreview-pack2x

The PR introduces two directories containing the exact same 5 schema files:

  • packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files, 1003 new lines)
  • packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files, 1003 new lines)

Every file is byte-identical across the two directories (verified with diff). This doubles the schema surface area without adding value. If models.py is updated and schemas are regenerated, both directories need updating or they silently drift.

Recommendation: Keep one canonical location (likely references/schemas/ since review prompts reference it) and either delete the other or replace it with a symlink/generation step.

packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/ReviewConcept.schema.json
Corroborated by: CHAD
B+sys.path.insert in tests bypasses proper packagingADtests review-pack1x

Both test_assembler.py and test_models.py use:

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "scripts"))

This is a pragmatic solution for a non-packaged script collection, and the production code (assemble_review_pack.py) uses the same pattern. So the test import path matches the production import path — this is honest, not gaming. However, it does mean that if the scripts directory is moved or restructured, tests could silently import the wrong module.

This is a minor structural concern, not a gaming pattern. The tests genuinely exercise real code paths.

packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_assembler.py
B+docs/research/ subdirectory not explicitly covered in zone registryARdocs1x

The file docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md is matched by the docs/*.md pattern only because Python's fnmatch treats * as matching /. Standard POSIX glob and pathlib.Path.match() do NOT match / with *. If the zone-matching implementation changes, this file would become unzoned.

Recommendation: Add docs/research/** to the docs zone paths, or to repo-infra alongside docs/proposals/**.

docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md
B+convert_gate0_tier2 is a dead stub called unconditionally in setup flowCHreview-pack1x

convert_gate0_tier2() in review_pack_setup.py (line 239-257) is called unconditionally during the setup flow (Step 5, line 315). The function iterates gate0 tier 2 files but only prints that conversion is 'not yet implemented'. It silently does nothing useful.

While it returns early if no tier 2 files exist (most cases), the stub creates confusion about whether gate0 conversion is a supported feature. Either implement the conversion or remove the call and document it as a future enhancement.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/review_pack_setup.py
B+check_prerequisites.py _check_playwright dead branch: chromium_ok unconditionally overwrittenCHreview-pack1x

In check_prerequisites.py lines 58-67:

chromium_ok = "chromium" not in (browsers.stdout or "").lower()
if not chromium_ok:
    test_result = subprocess.run(
        [npx, "playwright", "test", "--list"],
        ...
    )
    # If it can list tests, chromium is likely installed
    chromium_ok = True

When chromium_ok is False, the code runs playwright test --list but then unconditionally sets chromium_ok = True without checking test_result. The comment says 'if it can list tests, chromium is likely installed' but the code never checks the result. The subprocess call and conditional are dead logic — the outcome is always chromium_ok = True.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/check_prerequisites.py
B+run_deterministic_review.py uses bare callable type annotationCHreview-pack1x

At line 199 of run_deterministic_review.py:

tools_to_run: list[tuple[str, callable]] = []

callable (lowercase) is the builtin function, not a type annotation. While this works at runtime because Python doesn't enforce annotation types by default, it is incorrect for static type checking. mypy and other type checkers will flag this. The correct annotation would be Callable[[], dict] from typing or collections.abc.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/run_deterministic_review.py
B+Raw HTML rendering of detail_html fields from agent outputSEreview-pack1x

In render_review_pack.py, the detail_html field from ReviewConcept objects and the summary field from ArchitectureAssessmentOutput are rendered as raw HTML (not passed through html.escape). This is visible in multiple render functions where detail or summary content is inserted directly into the HTML output.

Risk assessment: Low severity. The review pack HTML is a local file opened in a browser. The data source is agent-produced .jsonl files within the local repository. There is no external user input path. The trust model is: agents produce HTML-safe content, pydantic validates structure, and the assembler validates semantics.

Mitigation already in place: All non-HTML fields (titles, file paths, zone IDs, labels) are correctly escaped via html.escape (aliased as esc()). The raw HTML rendering is limited to fields explicitly documented as "HTML-safe content" in the schema.

This is an acceptable design trade-off for a local tool. If the review pack were ever served over a network or the .jsonl files accepted external contributions, this would need sanitization (e.g., bleach/DOMPurify).

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/render_review_pack.py
▼Show 7 A-grade findings
ATests are substantive and non-vacuous — positive findingADtests review-pack1x

Adversarial review of test quality found no gaming patterns:

  • test_models.py: Tests both valid and invalid inputs through pydantic's real validation. Invalid grades (N/A, D) are rejected. Empty locations are rejected. Zone IDs are validated with regex. Title length constraints are enforced. These tests would all fail if the model validators were removed.
  • test_assembler.py: Tests use real tempfile I/O to write JSONL, then read and validate through the real pipeline. Transform tests verify actual output structure against real input models. Verification tests check real warning/error generation. The test_overall_grade_f_not_suppressed_by_c test catches a specific edge case in the grade collision logic.
  • E2E tests: Playwright tests assert against real DOM structure in a real browser. They verify section ordering, element visibility, and interactive behavior (click-to-expand).

No deletion test failures: removing any implementation would cause these tests to fail.

packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_assembler.py
AWell-structured 4-phase pipeline with clear component boundariesARreview-pack1x

The pipeline decomposition is architecturally sound:

  • Phase 1 (Setup): review_pack_setup.py orchestrates checkout, diff generation, scaffolding, and JSONL pre-creation.
  • Phase 2 (Review): 6 independent agents write JSONL files, each with a FileReviewOutcome per file plus ReviewConcept findings. Agent identity derived from filename, not data — clean design.
  • Phase 3 (Assemble): assemble_review_pack.py validates and transforms JSONL into ReviewPackData. models.py provides Pydantic validation as the enforcement layer.
  • Phase 4 (Deliver): render_review_pack.py produces the HTML artifact, with Playwright e2e tests verifying the output.

The separation between agent definitions (agents/) and paradigm prompts (packages/review-prompts/) correctly decouples agent identity/tooling from review methodology.

packages/pr-review-pack/docs/skill-flow.md
ACross-zone coupling between review-pack and review-prompts is intentional and well-boundedARreview-pack review-prompts1x

Agent definitions in packages/pr-review-pack/agents/ reference prompts via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/review-prompts/. The review-prompts zone is independently versioned and separately maintained. This PR updates both zones coherently: agent definitions point to the updated prompt format, and prompts now specify JSONL output with FileReviewOutcome requirements.

The coupling is narrow (path reference only) and the contract is clear (JSONL schema). This is healthy cross-zone interaction.

packages/pr-review-pack/agents/architecture-reviewer.md
ASubprocess usage follows security best practices throughoutSEreview-pack1x

The PR introduces substantial subprocess usage across multiple scripts:

  • check_prerequisites.py: Uses shutil.which() to resolve tool paths, then subprocess.run([path, version_flag], ...) with capture_output=True and timeout=10-15
  • run_deterministic_review.py: Uses [sys.executable, '-m', tool_name, ...] with timeout=120-180 and cwd=repo
  • review_pack_setup.py: Uses ['git', ...], ['gh', ...] with capture_output=True
  • scaffold_review_pack_data.py: Same pattern with ['gh', ...] and timeout=30

All calls correctly avoid shell=True. No user-supplied strings are interpolated into command arguments. Timeouts prevent resource exhaustion from hung processes. This is exemplary subprocess hygiene for a Python codebase.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/* (4 files)
AYAML parsing uses safe_load consistentlySEreview-pack1x

Both assemble_review_pack.py (line 826) and scaffold_review_pack_data.py (line 654) use yaml.safe_load() for parsing zone-registry.yaml. This is the correct approach — yaml.load() without a safe loader can execute arbitrary Python code via YAML tags like !!python/object.

No instances of unsafe yaml.load() were found anywhere in the PR.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/assemble_review_pack.py
AStrong model and assembler test coverage for new core modulesTItests1x

The two new test files are exemplary:

  • test_models.py tests all model types (ReviewConcept, SemanticOutput, FileReviewOutcome, ConceptUpdate), all valid/invalid grades, kebab-case validation, required fields, max/min lengths, enum variants, and cross-field validation (output_type must match populated field).
  • test_assembler.py tests filename parsing, ValidationReport, concept-to-finding transforms, grade sort ordering, semantic output transforms, verification checks (file-not-in-diff, zone-not-in-registry, duplicate IDs, coverage gaps), JSONL reading with real temp files, hybrid output parsing, ConceptUpdate merging, cascading validation, and architecture assessment degradation.

All assertions test meaningful properties of the system under test. The deletion test passes: replacing any tested function with pass would cause failures.

packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_models.py
AE2e test expansion is substantive and non-vacuousTIreview-pack1x

The e2e test expansion exercises the full rendering pipeline through Playwright against real HTML output. Notable additions include:

  • File Coverage table structure verification (header rows, per-agent columns, grade badges)
  • Key Findings section with severity grouping and corroboration badge rendering
  • Nav icon state verification (count-fail icons for C/F findings)
  • Modal interaction tests for file detail views

These tests are the opposite of vacuous — they load real rendered HTML, interact with DOM elements, and assert specific visible content. The deletion test holds: removing any rendering function would cause multiple e2e failures.

Research document. No code to game.
ARB+Architecture

The file docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md is matched by the docs/*.md pattern only because Python's fnmatch treats * as matching /. Standard POSIX glob and pathlib.Path.match() do NOT match / with *. If the zone-matching implementation changes, this file would become unzoned.

Recommendation: Add docs/research/** to the docs zone paths, or to repo-infra alongside docs/proposals/**.

This PR updates both zones coherently: agent definitions point to the updated prompt format, and prompts now specify JSONL output with FileReviewOutcome requirements.

The coupling is narrow (path reference only) and the contract is clear (JSONL schema). This is healthy cross-zone interaction.

packages/pr-review-pack/docs/skill-flow.mdAAAAA+339 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Documentation, no code health concerns.
SEASecurity
Documentation file, no security-relevant content.
TIATest Integrity
Documentation file, no test implications.
ADAAdversarial
Flow documentation. Honest representation of pipeline phases.
ARAArchitecture

The pipeline decomposition is architecturally sound:

  • Phase 1 (Setup): review_pack_setup.py orchestrates checkout, diff generation, scaffolding, and JSONL pre-creation.
  • Phase 2 (Review): 6 independent agents write JSONL files, each with a FileReviewOutcome per file plus ReviewConcept findings. Agent identity derived from filename, not data — clean design.
  • Phase 3 (Assemble): assemble_review_pack.py validates and transforms JSONL into ReviewPackData. models.py provides Pydantic validation as the enforcement layer.
  • Phase 4 (Deliver): render_review_pack.py produces the HTML artifact, with Playwright e2e tests verifying the output.

The separation between agent definitions (agents/) and paradigm prompts (packages/review-prompts/) correctly decouples agent identity/tooling from review methodology.

The e2e test expansion exercises the full rendering pipeline through Playwright against real HTML output. Notable additions include:

  • Key Findings section with severity grouping and corroboration badge rendering
  • Nav icon state verification (count-fail icons for C/F findings)
  • Modal interaction tests for file detail views

These tests are the opposite of vacuous — they load real rendered HTML, interact with DOM elements, and assert specific visible content. The deletion test holds: removing any rendering function would cause multiple e2e failures.

ADAAdversarial
E2E tests with real Playwright assertions against DOM structure. Tests exercise real browser rendering, not mocks. New Key Findings and File Coverage tests are substantive.

packages/pr-review-pack/install.shABBBA+74 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Clean install script with proper verification. No code health issues.
SEBSecurity

install.sh (lines 16-22): Uses rm -rf "$INSTALL_DIR" where INSTALL_DIR is hardcoded to $HOME/.claude/skills/pr-review-pack. This is safe — the path is a constant, not derived from user input. The script also checks whether the target is a symlink vs directory before choosing the removal method.

validate_skill.sh (lines 79-88): Uses rm -rf docs/reviews/ docs/*review_pack*.html zone-registry.yaml and git checkout -- . && git clean -fd inside cloned repo directories. The target directories are constructed from CLI-provided owner/repo:PR specs via dir_name=$(echo "$repo" | tr '/' '-')-${pr}. The IFS=: read -r repo pr pattern correctly splits the spec.

Additionally, validate_skill.sh Step 4 (lines 155-173) embeds $result_file and $dir_name into an inline Python script via shell interpolation. These variables are derived from the CLI spec and results directory, not from external input, so injection risk is negligible.

Risk assessment: Low. All destructive operations target paths that are either hardcoded constants or constructed from developer-provided CLI arguments in a local development context. The scripts use set -euo pipefail for fail-fast behavior. No untrusted input reaches these operations.

TIBTest Integrity
New 74-line install script with no test coverage. Contains environment detection and package installation logic that could fail silently.
ADBAdversarial

The install script has:

elif [ -d "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    echo "  Removing old installation..."
    rm -rf "$INSTALL_DIR"
fi

INSTALL_DIR is hardcoded to $HOME/.claude/skills/pr-review-pack, so the blast radius is limited. However, the script doesn't check that the directory actually contains a SKILL.md or other marker before deleting. If someone has a symlink at that path pointing elsewhere, the -L check catches symlinks but rm -rf on a real directory is unconditional.

This is not gaming — it's a minor safety gap in a developer-only tool. The verification step after installation is a good compensating control.

ARAArchitecture
New installer script for prerequisites. Good addition for setup automation.

SEASecurity

packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/ArchitectureAssessmentOutput.schema.jsonB+AAAB+300 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health
Exact duplicate of schemas/ directory content. See code-health-1 for details.
SEASecurity
JSON schema definition, no security-relevant content.
TIATest Integrity
Schema definition, validated indirectly through model tests.
ADAAdversarial
Schema definition. No adversarial patterns.
ARBArchitecture

Five schema files (ArchitectureAssessmentOutput, ConceptUpdate, FileReviewOutcome, ReviewConcept, SemanticOutput) are byte-identical copies in two directories. The references/schemas/ copy is referenced by agent paradigm prompts; the schemas/ copy appears to serve models.py and the assembler. If only one copy is updated in a future PR, schema divergence will cause silent validation mismatches.

Recommendation: Pick one canonical location and symlink or remove the other. Alternatively, have the Pydantic models in models.py export the schemas programmatically.

packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/ReviewConcept.schema.jsonB+AABB+133 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health

The PR introduces two directories containing the exact same 5 schema files:

  • packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files, 1003 new lines)
  • packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files, 1003 new lines)

Every file is byte-identical across the two directories (verified with diff). This doubles the schema surface area without adding value. If models.py is updated and schemas are regenerated, both directories need updating or they silently drift.

Recommendation: Keep one canonical location (likely references/schemas/ since review prompts reference it) and either delete the other or replace it with a symlink/generation step.

SEASecurity
JSON schema definition, no security-relevant content.
TIATest Integrity
Schema duplicate in schemas/ directory.
ADBAdversarial

The PR adds identical schema files to two locations:

  • packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files)
  • packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/*.schema.json (5 files)

The SKILL.md and reviewer prompts reference references/schemas/, but the schemas/ directory also exists. If a future change updates one copy but not the other, the pydantic models (which are the true enforcement layer) may diverge from the JSON schemas that agents read. This is not gaming, but it creates a subtle integrity gap: an agent could pass JSON schema validation against a stale copy while the assembler rejects it via pydantic.

Recommendation: use symlinks or a single canonical location.

ARBArchitecture
Exact duplicate of references/schemas/ version.

CHB+Code Health

TIATest Integrity

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/assemble_review_pack.pyB+AAAA+1006 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health
Well-structured assembler with clear phase separation. Minor: deferred import of scaffold_review_pack_data at line 894 and walrus operator in render block at line 987.
SEASecurity

Both assemble_review_pack.py (line 826) and scaffold_review_pack_data.py (line 654) use yaml.safe_load() for parsing zone-registry.yaml. This is the correct approach — yaml.load() without a safe loader can execute arbitrary Python code via YAML tags like !!python/object.

No instances of unsafe yaml.load() were found anywhere in the PR.

TIATest Integrity
New 1006-line assembler has thorough test coverage in test_assembler.py (744 lines). All major functions are tested: filename parsing, validation, transforms, JSONL reading, hybrid output, cascading validation, file coverage transform, and architecture degradation.
ADAAdversarial
Core assembler with real validation, real pydantic parsing, real transform logic. No hardcoded lookups or gaming patterns. Graceful degradation for architecture assessment is honest.
ARAArchitecture
New assembler: validates JSONL agent outputs, transforms into ReviewPackData. Core new component with clear single responsibility.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/check_prerequisites.pyB+ACAA+114 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health

In check_prerequisites.py lines 58-67:

chromium_ok = "chromium" not in (browsers.stdout or "").lower()
if not chromium_ok:
    test_result = subprocess.run(
        [npx, "playwright", "test", "--list"],
        ...
    )
    # If it can list tests, chromium is likely installed
    chromium_ok = True

When chromium_ok is False, the code runs playwright test --list but then unconditionally sets chromium_ok = True without checking test_result. The comment says 'if it can list tests, chromium is likely installed' but the code never checks the result. The subprocess call and conditional are dead logic — the outcome is always chromium_ok = True.

SEASecurity
Subprocess calls use list-form arguments with shell=False (default). Commands are hardcoded tool names resolved via shutil.which. No user input reaches command arguments.
TICTest Integrity
New 114-line script with no test coverage. Contains environment checking logic (Python version, pydantic availability, Playwright) that could be unit tested.
ADAAdversarial
Real subprocess calls to check tool availability. No mocked or faked checks.
ARAArchitecture
Prerequisite checker for the Setup phase. Clean utility.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/generate_diff_data.pyAAAAA+3 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Clean diff data generator with clear single-responsibility functions.
SEASecurity
Minor diff change (+3 lines). Existing script uses subprocess with list-form arguments. No security regression.
TIATest Integrity
Minimal 3-line addition, existing tests adequate.
ADAAdversarial
Minimal 3-line change. No adversarial patterns.
ARAArchitecture
Minor modification to existing diff generator.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/inspect_session.pyBACB+A+793 −0review-pack
CHBCode Health

In check_filesystem_artifacts at line 605:

"html_found": len(html_files) > 0 if 'html_files' in dir() else False,

This uses 'html_files' in dir() as a scope guard, which is a code smell. The variable html_files is only assigned inside the first if html_files: block (line 551), but it is actually always assigned at line 549 via list((repo_dir / "docs").glob(html_pattern)), so the guard is vestigial. However, using dir() for variable existence checks is fragile and non-Pythonic. A simple initialization like html_found = False at the top of the function would be clearer.

SEASecurity
Pure read-only analysis of local session JSONL files. No subprocess calls, no network access, no file writes. Parses JSON safely via json.loads.
TICTest Integrity

inspect_session.py is 793 lines of validation logic that serves as a quality gate for review sessions. It contains:

  • JSONL file discovery and parsing
  • Schema validation against Pydantic models
  • File coverage completeness checks
  • Concept backing verification (non-A grades need backing concepts)
  • Zone registry cross-referencing
  • Synthesis content validation

While some of this logic overlaps with assemble_review_pack.py (which is tested), the inspector has its own implementation with distinct error reporting. Without tests, regressions in the inspector could silently pass broken review sessions through the factory.

The assembler tests cover similar concepts but through different code paths — this is not a substitute for testing the inspector directly.

ADB+Adversarial
Session inspector with 12 checks. Uses real JSONL parsing. Some checks rely on string matching (e.g., agent name detection in prompts) which could produce false positives, but this is pragmatic not gaming.
ARAArchitecture
New session inspector with 12 checks. Well-structured diagnostic tool for validating skill execution.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/models.pyAAABA+605 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Clean pydantic model definitions with proper validation, good enum usage, and clear type hierarchy.
SEASecurity
Pydantic model definitions with strict validation. Input validation via field_validator decorators enforces kebab-case zone IDs and grade enums. No I/O operations.
TIATest Integrity
New 605-line Pydantic model file with excellent test coverage in test_models.py (489 lines). Tests cover all model types, validation rules, grade ordering, edge cases, and example JSONL parsing.
ADBAdversarial

In models.py, the legacy grade sort order is:

LEGACY_GRADE_SORT_ORDER = {Grade.F: 0, Grade.C: 0, Grade.B: 1, Grade.B_PLUS: 2, Grade.A: 3}

The assembler's transform_concepts_to_review() correctly handles this by using a secondary _GRADE_SEVERITY dict to break ties. The test test_overall_grade_f_not_suppressed_by_c explicitly validates this edge case. So the implementation is correct — but the dual-sort-order design is fragile.

If anyone uses LEGACY_GRADE_SORT_ORDER directly for sorting (without the secondary tiebreaker), F-grade findings could sort after C-grade findings or vice versa depending on insertion order. The fix is documented in test, which is good. Not gaming, but a latent integrity risk.

ARAArchitecture
Pydantic model layer defining all structured output schemas. Clean separation of data model from business logic. Single source of truth for validation.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/render_review_pack.pyBB+BAA+399 −18review-pack
CHBCode Health

The renderer builds complex HTML structures via Python f-string concatenation across multiple functions. Two functions stand out:

  • render_architecture_svg (lines 157-266, ~110 lines): Constructs SVG elements with inline style attributes, onclick handlers, and nested text/tspan elements.
  • render_architecture_assessment (lines 269-447, ~180 lines): Builds nested collapsible sections with tables, badges, and subsections via string list concatenation.

This pattern is functionally correct but creates a maintenance burden: HTML structure is interleaved with Python logic, making it difficult to verify tag balance, attribute quoting, or CSS class correctness. The template_v2.html file uses injection markers which partially addresses this, but the section renderers themselves are still pure string construction.

This is not a bug but a structural complexity that increases the risk of regressions in future changes to the HTML output.

SEB+Security

In render_review_pack.py, the detail_html field from ReviewConcept objects and the summary field from ArchitectureAssessmentOutput are rendered as raw HTML (not passed through html.escape). This is visible in multiple render functions where detail or summary content is inserted directly into the HTML output.

Risk assessment: Low severity. The review pack HTML is a local file opened in a browser. The data source is agent-produced .jsonl files within the local repository. There is no external user input path. The trust model is: agents produce HTML-safe content, pydantic validates structure, and the assembler validates semantics.

Mitigation already in place: All non-HTML fields (titles, file paths, zone IDs, labels) are correctly escaped via html.escape (aliased as esc()). The raw HTML rendering is limited to fields explicitly documented as "HTML-safe content" in the schema.

This is an acceptable design trade-off for a local tool. If the review pack were ever served over a network or the .jsonl files accepted external contributions, this would need sanitization (e.g., bleach/DOMPurify).

TIBTest Integrity

The renderer expansion adds three significant new functions:

  • render_key_findings() — groups findings by severity, renders corroboration badges, collapses A-grade findings
  • _detect_corroboration() — detects cross-agent corroboration via file overlap and title similarity
  • render_file_coverage_table() — renders per-agent grade columns for each file

The e2e tests in review-pack-v2.spec.ts verify these render correctly in the browser, which is valuable. However, unit tests would catch edge cases: empty findings list, single agent with no corroboration possible, title similarity false positives, and grade sorting with mixed severity.

The existing test_renderer_sidebar.py and test_renderer_integration.py updates only cover the nav rename from 'Code Review' to 'Key Findings'/'File Coverage' — they do not test the new rendering logic.

ADAAdversarial
Real rendering logic for Key Findings, File Coverage, corroboration detection. No hardcoded outputs.
ARAArchitecture
Substantially expanded renderer with new sections (key findings, architecture SVG, sidebar enhancements). Largest single file change in the PR.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/review_pack_setup.pyB+BCAA+338 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health

convert_gate0_tier2() in review_pack_setup.py (line 239-257) is called unconditionally during the setup flow (Step 5, line 315). The function iterates gate0 tier 2 files but only prints that conversion is 'not yet implemented'. It silently does nothing useful.

While it returns early if no tier 2 files exist (most cases), the stub creates confusion about whether gate0 conversion is a supported feature. Either implement the conversion or remove the call and document it as a future enhancement.

SEBSecurity
GraphQL query in check_prerequisites interpolates owner/name from git remote URL into a query string. The values are constrained by GitHub repo naming rules but are not parameterized. Subprocess calls correctly use list-form with shell=False.
TICTest Integrity

review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) handles:

  • Review directory creation and JSONL file scaffolding
  • Meta header generation for agent output files
  • Diff data generation orchestration

check_prerequisites.py (114 lines) handles:

  • Python version detection
  • Pydantic availability checking
  • Playwright installation verification

Both scripts perform I/O and environment detection that could be tested with tmp_path fixtures and mock environments. Without tests, silent failures in setup could produce empty or malformed JSONL files that downstream tools fail to process.

ADAAdversarial
Consolidated setup script with real git/gh calls. No gaming patterns.
ARAArchitecture
New Phase 1 orchestrator: PR checkout, diff generation, scaffolding, JSONL pre-creation. Clean pipeline entry point.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/run_deterministic_review.pyB+ABAA+272 −0review-pack
CHB+Code Health

At line 199 of run_deterministic_review.py:

tools_to_run: list[tuple[str, callable]] = []

callable (lowercase) is the builtin function, not a type annotation. While this works at runtime because Python doesn't enforce annotation types by default, it is incorrect for static type checking. mypy and other type checkers will flag this. The correct annotation would be Callable[[], dict] from typing or collections.abc.

SEASecurity
All subprocess calls use list-form arguments with sys.executable (shell=False). Timeout limits are set on all calls. cwd parameter uses a resolved Path. No user-supplied strings reach commands.
TIBTest Integrity
New 272-line script with no dedicated tests. As an orchestration script that calls other tested components, the risk is lower but error handling paths are untested.
ADAAdversarial
Runs real tools (vulture, bandit, ruff, mypy) via subprocess. Parallel execution with ThreadPoolExecutor. No faked tool outputs.
ARAArchitecture
Gate 0 Tier 1 deterministic review runner (ruff, mypy, bandit, vulture). Parallel execution via ThreadPoolExecutor.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/scaffold_review_pack_data.pyB+BBAA+116 −77review-pack
CHB+Code Health
Well-structured scaffold builder. The scaffold function at line 647 is long (260 lines) but mostly sequential data assembly which is acceptable for its role.
SEBSecurity

In scaffold_review_pack_data.py (lines 691-699) and review_pack_setup.py (lines 85-93), the GitHub owner and repo name are extracted from git remote get-url origin and interpolated directly into GraphQL query strings:

query=f'{{ repository(owner: "{owner}", name: "{name}") {{ ... }} }}'

Risk assessment: Low severity in this context. The values come from the local git remote URL, which is controlled by the repository configuration. GitHub's repository naming rules ([a-zA-Z0-9._-]) constrain the character set, making injection impractical. The code in scaffold_review_pack_data.py (line 688) documents this trust assumption explicitly.

Nevertheless, the idiomatic approach is to use GraphQL variables ($owner: String!) passed via the -F flag to gh api graphql, which would eliminate the interpolation entirely and be more robust against edge cases (e.g., repo names containing quotes, which GitHub currently disallows but the code doesn't validate).

TIBTest Integrity
Significant refactor (+116/-77) with no dedicated test updates. Existing integration tests may cover happy path but refactored logic is not directly validated.
ADAAdversarial
Scaffold script with real diff data integration. No adversarial patterns.
ARAArchitecture
Refactored scaffolder with zone-aware data generation. Supports new pipeline structure.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/validate_skill.shABBAA+290 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Clean bash script with proper set -euo pipefail and clear structure.
SEBSecurity
Shell script uses set -euo pipefail. Variable expansions in commands are mostly safe (repo specs from CLI args flow into gh commands and directory paths). rm -rf is used on known paths. Python heredoc in Step 4 interpolates $result_file and $dir_name from controlled variables.
TIBTest Integrity

Four scripts in this PR have B-grade test coverage gaps:

  • validate_skill.sh (290 lines) — shell script running validation checks across the review pack pipeline. Contains conditional logic for check sequencing and error aggregation with no test harness.
  • install.sh (74 lines) — environment detection and package installation. Silent failures here could leave the skill non-functional.
  • run_deterministic_review.py (272 lines) — orchestrates deterministic review steps by calling other scripts. The called scripts are individually tested but the orchestration error paths are not.
  • scaffold_review_pack_data.py (refactored, +116/-77) — significant logic changes to scaffolding with no test updates. Existing integration tests may catch gross failures but refactored branching logic is unvalidated.

These are graded B rather than C because they are primarily orchestration scripts that delegate to tested components. The untested surface is control flow and error handling, not core business logic.

ADAAdversarial
Real end-to-end validation harness using claude -p. No gaming patterns.
ARAArchitecture
New validation script for skill installation and configuration checks.
packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_assembler.pyAAAB+A+744 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Thorough test suite covering validation, transforms, and edge cases. Good use of helper methods for test data construction.
SEASecurity
Test file with no security-relevant patterns. Uses tmp_path fixtures for file operations.
TIATest Integrity
Excellent new test file (744 lines). Tests are substantive: they construct real Pydantic models, exercise real transform logic, verify correct outputs with meaningful assertions. Tests cover edge cases (empty inputs, duplicates, missing IDs, zone validation, grade ordering). Integration tests use real temp files for JSONL reading.
ADB+Adversarial

Both test_assembler.py and test_models.py use:

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "scripts"))

This is a pragmatic solution for a non-packaged script collection, and the production code (assemble_review_pack.py) uses the same pattern. So the test import path matches the production import path — this is honest, not gaming. However, it does mean that if the scripts directory is moved or restructured, tests could silently import the wrong module.

This is a minor structural concern, not a gaming pattern. The tests genuinely exercise real code paths.

Adversarial review of test quality found no gaming patterns:

  • test_models.py: Tests both valid and invalid inputs through pydantic's real validation. Invalid grades (N/A, D) are rejected. Empty locations are rejected. Zone IDs are validated with regex. Title length constraints are enforced. These tests would all fail if the model validators were removed.
  • test_assembler.py: Tests use real tempfile I/O to write JSONL, then read and validate through the real pipeline. Transform tests verify actual output structure against real input models. Verification tests check real warning/error generation. The test_overall_grade_f_not_suppressed_by_c test catches a specific edge case in the grade collision logic.
  • E2E tests: Playwright tests assert against real DOM structure in a real browser. They verify section ordering, element visibility, and interactive behavior (click-to-expand).

No deletion test failures: removing any implementation would cause these tests to fail.

ARAArchitecture
Comprehensive test suite for the new assembler (744 lines). Tests validation, transformation, and edge cases.
packages/pr-review-pack/tests/test_models.pyAAAAA+489 −0review-pack
CHACode Health
Comprehensive model validation tests covering all grades, categories, edge cases, and error conditions.
SEASecurity
Test file with no security-relevant patterns. Tests pydantic model validation.
TIATest Integrity

The two new test files are exemplary:

  • test_models.py tests all model types (ReviewConcept, SemanticOutput, FileReviewOutcome, ConceptUpdate), all valid/invalid grades, kebab-case validation, required fields, max/min lengths, enum variants, and cross-field validation (output_type must match populated field).
  • test_assembler.py tests filename parsing, ValidationReport, concept-to-finding transforms, grade sort ordering, semantic output transforms, verification checks (file-not-in-diff, zone-not-in-registry, duplicate IDs, coverage gaps), JSONL reading with real temp files, hybrid output parsing, ConceptUpdate merging, cascading validation, and architecture assessment degradation.

All assertions test meaningful properties of the system under test. The deletion test passes: replacing any tested function with pass would cause failures.

ADAAdversarial
489-line test file thoroughly exercises pydantic validation. Tests verify both valid and invalid inputs. No mocking, no stubs, no tautological assertions.
ARAArchitecture
Thorough Pydantic model tests (489 lines). Covers validation rules, grade enums, zone patterns.

packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/* (4 files)—A———+0 −0
CH—Code Health
No comments on this file.
SEASecurity

The PR introduces substantial subprocess usage across multiple scripts:

  • check_prerequisites.py: Uses shutil.which() to resolve tool paths, then subprocess.run([path, version_flag], ...) with capture_output=True and timeout=10-15
  • run_deterministic_review.py: Uses [sys.executable, '-m', tool_name, ...] with timeout=120-180 and cwd=repo
  • review_pack_setup.py: Uses ['git', ...], ['gh', ...] with capture_output=True
  • scaffold_review_pack_data.py: Same pattern with ['gh', ...] and timeout=30

All calls correctly avoid shell=True. No user-supplied strings are interpolated into command arguments. Timeouts prevent resource exhaustion from hung processes. This is exemplary subprocess hygiene for a Python codebase.

TI—Test Integrity
No comments on this file.
AD—Adversarial
No comments on this file.
AR—Architecture
No comments on this file.

▼ Follow-ups

Post-Merge Items

▼
MEDIUM Duplicate schema directories will silently drift

All 5 JSON schema files exist identically in both packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/ and packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/. Agent paradigm prompts reference the references/schemas/ copies. If either directory is updated independently, agents could validate against stale schemas while the Pydantic models enforce different rules. Corroborated by 3 agents: adversarial (B), architecture (B), and code-health (B+) all independently flagged this as a maintenance risk.

Failure scenario
A future PR updates models.py and regenerates schemas in one directory but not the other. Agents read the stale schema, produce output that passes JSON schema validation but fails Pydantic validation in the assembler, causing cryptic assembly failures.
Resolution
One canonical schema location is chosen (references/schemas/ since prompts reference it). The other directory is removed or replaced with symlinks. A CI check or generation script ensures schemas match Pydantic model exports.
review-pack
MEDIUM <code>inspect_session.py</code> (793 lines) has zero test coverage

The session inspector is a critical quality gate with 12 validation checks including JSONL parsing, schema validation, file coverage enforcement, and concept backing verification. It is the largest new script in the PR and has no corresponding test file. Corroborated by 2 agents: test-integrity graded it C (zero coverage), code-health graded it B (complexity concerns with 94-line functions and fragile dir() scope guards).

## packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/inspect_session.py, lines 605 "html_found": len(html_files) > 0 if 'html_files' in dir() else False,
Failure scenario
A regression in the inspector silently passes broken review sessions through the factory. Since the inspector shares logic patterns with the assembler but has its own implementation, bugs can hide in the inspector-specific code paths that assembler tests do not cover.
Resolution
A test_inspect_session.py file exercises all 12 checks with known-good and known-bad session data, catching regressions in the inspector independently of assembler tests.
review-pack
MEDIUM Setup and prerequisite scripts (452 lines combined) untested

review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) and check_prerequisites.py (114 lines) handle Phase 1 orchestration and environment detection with no test coverage. Test-integrity graded both C. Code-health identified a dead branch in _check_playwright where chromium_ok is unconditionally set to True regardless of the subprocess result, and a stub function convert_gate0_tier2() that prints 'not yet implemented' but is called unconditionally in the main flow.

## packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/check_prerequisites.py, lines 58-67 chromium_ok = "chromium" not in (browsers.stdout or "").lower() if not chromium_ok: test_result = subprocess.run([npx, "playwright", "test", "--list"], ...) chromium_ok = True # unconditionally overwritten
Failure scenario
Silent setup failures produce empty or malformed JSONL scaffolding files. Downstream agents write into corrupted files, and the assembler fails with opaque errors that don't trace back to the setup phase.
Resolution
Unit tests with tmp_path fixtures validate scaffolding logic. The dead chromium_ok branch is fixed to check test_result.returncode. The convert_gate0_tier2 stub is either implemented or removed with a TODO comment.
review-pack
LOW New renderer functions lack unit tests for edge cases

The renderer gained 399 lines including render_key_findings(), _detect_corroboration(), and render_file_coverage_table(). These have E2E Playwright coverage but no unit tests for edge cases like empty findings, single-agent corroboration, or title similarity false positives. Test-integrity graded this B.

Failure scenario
Edge case in corroboration detection produces incorrect badges (false positive corroboration or missed true corroboration). E2E tests only cover the happy path with pre-built test data.
Resolution
Unit tests exercise _detect_corroboration with edge cases: zero findings, single agent, identical titles from same agent (not corroboration), similar-but-distinct titles across agents.
review-pack
LOW GraphQL queries use f-string interpolation instead of parameterized variables

Both scaffold_review_pack_data.py and review_pack_setup.py interpolate GitHub owner/name from git remote URLs directly into GraphQL query strings. Security reviewer graded this B. The risk is low since values are constrained by GitHub naming rules, but the idiomatic approach uses GraphQL variables via gh api graphql -F flags.

## packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/scaffold_review_pack_data.py, lines 691-699 query=f'{{ repository(owner: "{owner}", name: "{name}") {{ ... }} }}'
Failure scenario
A repository name containing unexpected characters (hypothetically, if GitHub naming rules change) causes GraphQL injection or query parse failure.
Resolution
Both scripts use gh api graphql -F owner=$owner -F name=$name with $owner/$name GraphQL variables, eliminating string interpolation entirely.
review-pack
